title: RC-007: Fix Race in stop() Concurrent Execution (.6)
description:
## Description
Race condition in stop() allows concurrent execution (bug .6). The stop() method in the engine lacks proper synchronization, allowing new work to begin while the engine is shutting down. This can result in tasks being started after resources have been released, causing undefined behavior, crashes, or resource leaks.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Add shutdown state machine with proper locking | Prevent new work during stop sequence |
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Use atomic state transitions | Ensure single-threaded shutdown logic |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] stop() prevents any new task execution once initiated
- [ ] Atomic state check-and-set for running -> stopping transition
- [ ] In-progress operations complete before shutdown finishes
- [ ] No new work accepted during or after stop() called
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed stop() race condition by using stateLock for atomic operation. Added stopInProgress flag to prevent concurrent execution. stop() now prevents new iterations during shutdown.

title: RC-004: Fix Worker Reservation and Claim Race (.7)
description:
## Description
Race condition in worker reservation and claim (bug .7). Workers reserve capacity before claiming tasks, but the reservation and claim are not performed atomically. This allows another worker to observe the reserved capacity as available and attempt a concurrent claim, potentially overallocating resources or causing task assignment conflicts.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Bundle reservation and claim in atomic transaction | Prevent reservation-claim split race |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Worker reservation and task claim are atomic operation
- [ ] No window between reserving capacity and claiming task
- [ ] Capacity counters remain consistent under concurrent load
- [ ] Unit tests verify proper serialization under contention
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed worker reservation race - worker reservation and task claiming are now atomic under state lock

title: [Coordinator]: Fire-and-forget void calls lose error context
description:
**Bug:** Fire-and-forget void calls lose error context

**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts

**Issue:** Void-returning fire-and-forget calls don't propagate errors to caller, losing error context and making debugging difficult.

**Impact:** Errors silently dropped, failures go unnoticed, debugging is difficult.

**Fix:** Capture and log errors, add error callbacks, or return promises for async tracking.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Fix fire-and-forget error handling | Add error capture/logging |

title: [Engine]: Subagent parser event handlers never removed - memory leak
description:
**Bug:** Subagent parser event handlers never removed - memory leak

**Location:** src/engine/index.ts:225-228, 996-998

**Issue:** SubagentTraceParser event handlers never removed, leaking memory through closures.

**Impact:** Memory leak of engine state, events from old iterations affect new ones.

**Fix:** Add dispose() method to remove handlers, or create new parser per iteration.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Fix subagent parser memory leak | Add handler cleanup |

title: [Async]: Fire-and-forget engine.stop() loses interrupt errors
description:
**Location:** src/remote/server.ts:776-790

**Pattern:** 


**Issue:** Engine stop operation is fire-and-forget - no await, response is sent asynchronously without guaranteed ordering. Race conditions possible where multiple interrupt requests interleave.

**Impact:** Client may receive response out of order, or error may be lost if WebSocket closes before error callback fires. Can cause client to think interrupt succeeded when it failed.

**Fix:** Make handler async and await the stop() operation, or use a request-response correlation queue.

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in parallel logging infrastructure
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:162-164, 182-184, 254-255, 275-276, 286-287, 2276-2277

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Multiple locations in parallel coordinator silently ignore logging and summary failures. These are critical for debugging parallel execution issues.

**Impact:** When parallel execution fails, the logs needed to diagnose the issue may be missing. This creates a double failure scenario.

**Fix:** Log logging failures to stderr as a last resort, or maintain an in-memory buffer of recent events that can be dumped on catastrophic failure.

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in WebSocket client reconnection
description:
**Location:** src/remote/client.ts:1045-1048

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** WebSocket creation failures during reconnection are completely silent. No logging of why reconnection is failing.

**Impact:** Reconnection loops can occur indefinitely without any indication of the root cause, making network/debugging issues impossible to diagnose.

**Fix:** Log WebSocket creation failures with error details and connection state before scheduling reconnect.

title: [Data-Consistency]: Template cache race condition in engine.ts
description:
**Bug:** Template cache race condition in engine.ts

**Location:** src/engine/index.ts

**Issue:** Template cache accessed from multiple concurrent operations without proper synchronization.

**Impact:** Race conditions, corrupted cache state, inconsistent template data.

**Fix:** Add synchronization around cache access, use read-write locks or atomic updates.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Fix template cache race | Add synchronization |

title: [Async]: Unhandled dispose() errors in registry reset
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/registry.ts:86,113, src/plugins/trackers/registry.ts:86,113

**Pattern:** 


**Issue:** Registry reset() fires multiple async dispose() operations concurrently without tracking. Uses .catch() for logging but errors still fire asynchronously. Potential race condition where registry is cleared before dispose() completes.

**Impact:** Plugin instances may still be disposing after registry claims to be reset. Resources may not be properly cleaned up. Tests may fail intermittently due to race between reset() and subsequent operations.

**Fix:** Await all dispose() calls with Promise.all() before clearing registry state.

title: [Config]: Type coercion in Droid output parser without validation
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/droid/outputParser.ts:694 and readNumber function

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** The  coercion doesn't validate input format. Strings like '1e2', '0x10', empty strings, and invalid formats can produce unexpected results.  check catches some cases but allows through values like 'Infinity', '-Infinity'.

**Impact:** Silent data corruption in token/cost calculations, exit codes, and status codes parsed from agent output.

**Fix:** Use  or  with explicit regex validation before parsing. Reject non-finite values.

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in remote instance manager connection
description:
**Location:** src/remote/instance-manager.ts:262-264

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Connection errors are silently swallowed with only a comment claiming handling is elsewhere. No local error context is preserved.

**Impact:** Connection failures may not be properly tracked if the event handler doesn't fire or fires with incomplete information.

**Fix:** Log connection attempt failures locally before delegating to event handler, preserving error context for debugging.

title: [Config]: Port parsing allows invalid values through type coercion
description:
**Location:** src/remote/config.ts:226-253 (parseHostPort function)

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Uses regex  to validate before parseInt, but the pattern still allows leading zeros (e.g., '0080' which could be interpreted as octal in some contexts, though parseInt with radix 10 prevents this). More critically, the regex validation happens but doesn't reject strings like '0000' which results in port 0 (invalid).

**Impact:** Could allow configuration of invalid port numbers that fail silently at connection time.

**Fix:** Explicitly reject port 0 and ensure no leading zeros unless the port is actually 0 (which should be rejected).

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in log event files
description:
**Location:** src/logs/parallel-events.ts:49-51, src/logs/run-summary.ts:284-286

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Event logging failures are silently swallowed in multiple log files. These are the primary diagnostic tools.

**Impact:** When troubleshooting issues, the very logs needed may be missing with no indication they failed to write.

**Fix:** At minimum log write failures to stderr, or maintain fallback in-memory logging for critical events.

title: [Data-Consistency]: Plugin registry cached instances lack freshness checks
description:
**Bug:** Plugin registry cached instances lack freshness checks

**Location:** src/plugins/registry.ts

**Issue:** Cached plugin instances don't have freshness checks, may return stale data.

**Impact:** Stale plugin state, inconsistent behavior, outdated configurations.

**Fix:** Add TTL or version checks to cached instances, validate freshness on access.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/plugins/registry.ts | modify | Add freshness checks | TTL/version validation |

title: [Coordinator]: Missing cleanup of mergeWorker in attemptMergeResolution
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:2517-2520, 2551-2558

**Issue:** In , a  is created (line 2518) and disposed (line 2520), but if an error occurs between creation and disposal, or if  itself fails, the agent instance may not be properly cleaned up. The worktree cleanup has error handling but the worker disposal doesn't.

**Impact:** Agent process/subagent leaks during merge conflict resolution failures.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use try/finally to ensure dispose() is always called, or track all created agents for cleanup during coordinator shutdown.

title: [Async]: Race condition in task lease check-then-act
description:
**Bug:** Race condition in task lease check-then-act

**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts

**Issue:** Task lease check and act are separate non-atomic operations, allowing race conditions.

**Impact:** Double leasing, task conflicts, inconsistent lease state.

**Fix:** Make lease check-and-act atomic, use compare-and-swap pattern.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Fix task lease race | Atomic check-and-act |

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in session reconciliation logging
description:
**Location:** src/session/reconciliation.ts:384-386

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Reconciliation report logging failures are silently ignored. Reconciliation is a critical session integrity operation.

**Impact:** Session corruption or inconsistencies may go undetected if reconciliation reports are lost.

**Fix:** Log reconciliation failures at error level - this is a critical operation that should never fail silently.

title: [Data-Consistency]: Store read-then-update race in event bridge
description:
**Bug:** Store read-then-update race in event bridge

**Location:** src/engine/event-bridge.ts

**Issue:** Store read followed by update is not atomic, allowing race conditions in event bridge.

**Impact:** Lost updates, inconsistent store state, event processing errors.

**Fix:** Use atomic read-modify-write operations, add versioning for optimistic locking.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/event-bridge.ts | modify | Fix store read-update race | Atomic operations |

title: [Async]: Race condition in merge queue check-then-act
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:2333

**Pattern:** 


**Issue:** TOCTOU race condition: merge key is checked, then entry is pushed to queue, then key is added. Between check and add, another task could process same merge key.

**Impact:** Duplicate merge entries for same commit, wasted work, potential merge conflicts.

**Fix:** Use atomic add-and-check pattern: attempt to add key first, check return value to see if it was newly added.

description:
**Location:** src/session/persistence.ts:265-280, 577-628

**Issue:** Session file is read, modified in memory, then written back with no locking mechanism. If another process reads the file between the read and write, it gets stale data and its subsequent write will overwrite the first modification (lost update).

**Impact:** Session state corruption on concurrent operations; crash recovery may reset tasks that were actually completed; iteration counts and task statuses can become inconsistent.

**Fix:** Implement file-level locking (e.g., flock) or atomic write-then-rename pattern with conflict detection.

title: [Remote]: Unbounded connection token accumulation - memory DoS risk
description:
**Location:** src/remote/token.ts:317, 323-334, 405-411

**Issue:** The activeConnectionTokens Map has no size limit. If clients repeatedly connect without proper disconnect (e.g., network issues, crashes), tokens accumulate indefinitely since cleanup only happens on explicit disconnect or periodic expiry cleanup.

**Impact:** Memory exhaustion leading to server crash (DoS). Each token stores ~200 bytes plus Map overhead. With 10k abandoned connections, this could consume several MB of heap that is never freed until server restart.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add a maximum size limit to activeConnectionTokens (e.g., 1000 tokens). Implement LRU eviction or reject new connections when limit is reached. Track token creation time for more aggressive cleanup of old tokens even if not technically expired.

title: [Performance] Memory churn from repeated TextEncoder and Map allocations in output-buffer
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/output-buffer.ts:46-58, 144-231

**Pattern:** Memory Churn - Excessive Object Creation

Multiple performance issues in output buffer state management:
1. Line 51: `new TextEncoder()` created on every `trimToCap` call
2. Lines 148, 157, 170, 179, 189: New Map created on every output update
3. Lines 62-63: `cloneSegments` creates shallow copies that may not be needed
4. Line 52: `encoder.encode(output)` allocates byte array for entire output

**Impact:**
- GC pressure from repeated object allocations
- Memory fragmentation from frequent Map creation
- Unnecessary encoding of potentially large output strings

**Fix:**
1. Move TextEncoder to module-level singleton
2. Use Map mutation instead of creating new Maps: `nextOutputs.set()` on cloned Map instead of `new Map(state.parallelOutputs)`
3. Implement lazy segment cloning - only clone when segments actually change
4. Consider byte-length estimation instead of full encoding for size checks

**Expected Gain:** 20-40% reduction in GC pauses during high-volume output streaming

title: [Performance] Sequential snapshot validation and rotation blocks cleanup
description:
**Location:** src/snapshot/index.ts:309-364 (rotateSnapshots function)

**Pattern:** Sequential Operations in Cleanup Path

The snapshot rotation function processes files sequentially:
- Lines 330-338: Sequential `for...of` loop with `await isValidSnapshot()` for each file
- Lines 346-352: Sequential deletion loop with `await unlink()` per snapshot
- Lines 377-396: Similar pattern in `listSnapshots`

**Impact:**
- Cleanup operations scale linearly with snapshot count
- I/O operations could be parallelized for faster rotation
- Blocks other operations during snapshot maintenance

**Fix:**
1. Batch validation using Promise.all or Promise.allSettled
2. Parallel deletion with controlled concurrency (p-limit or similar)
3. Consider background/queue-based cleanup for non-blocking operation
4. Use fs/promises.readdir with withFileTypes for atomic metadata

**Expected Gain:** 50-70% faster snapshot rotation with high snapshot counts

title: [Coordinator]: Merge queue idempotency issues on failure
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:2343-2356, 2358-2433

**Issue:** `enqueueMerge` creates merge queue entries and immediately calls `void this.processMergeQueue()`. If processing fails, the merge key remains in `queuedMergeKeys` but the entry is removed from queue. On retry, duplicate merge entries could be created or commits could be lost.

**Impact:** Duplicate commits in merge history, or lost commits when merge fails but commit is marked processed.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
void this.enqueueMerge({...}); // Fire and forget
// In processMergeQueue:
this.queuedMergeKeys.delete(...); // Removed on failure
// But processedCommits is NOT added on failure, so retry will re-queue
```

**Fix Guidance:** Track merge attempts separately from successful processing, ensure idempotency of merge operations.

title: [Coordinator]: Task may stay blocked after main sync success
[Remote]: Connection token validation not constant-time
description:
**Location:** src/remote/token.ts:340-365

**Issue:** The validateConnectionToken function uses Map.get() for token lookup which is not constant-time. While Map lookups are generally fast, the timing can vary based on hash collisions and bucket traversal, creating a timing side channel.

**Impact:** Attackers with precise timing capabilities could potentially distinguish between 'token not found' and 'token found but expired' responses, gaining information about valid token hashes.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** For high-security scenarios, implement a constant-time token validation by comparing against all stored tokens in a fixed iteration pattern, or use a cryptographic MAC verification approach where the timing is independent of the token value.

title: [Engine]: refreshTasks single-flight can lose refresh requests
description:
**Location:** src/engine/index.ts:439-477

**Issue:** The refreshTasks method implements single-flight with queuing, but the queue check happens BEFORE setting refreshInFlight:


If refreshTasks is called rapidly in succession:
1. Call 1 starts, sets refreshInFlight = true
2. Call 2 arrives, sees refreshInFlight, sets refreshQueued = true, returns
3. Call 1 completes, checks refreshQueued (true), loops, resets refreshQueued = false, processes
4. Call 3 arrives, sees refreshInFlight is now false, starts immediately

This is mostly correct but there's a window where refreshQueued could be overwritten.

**Impact:** Minor - could miss a refresh request in very high concurrency scenarios.

**Pattern:** Simple boolean flag instead of proper queue.

**Fix Guidance:** Use a counter or proper queue to track multiple pending refreshes, or switch to a debounce/throttle pattern.

title: [Plugins]: Unbounded taskCache growth in BeadsTrackerPlugin causes memory leak
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:259, 575-607

**Issue:** The taskCache Map (line 259) is populated in getTask() (line 605) but never cleared. Long-running sessions with many task lookups will consume unbounded memory.

**Impact:** Memory leak in long-running ralph-tui sessions, especially when iterating through many beads.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add cache size limit with LRU eviction, or clear cache on sync() operations.

title: [Remote]: Race condition in broadcastEngineEvent - stale WebSocket state
description:
**Location:** src/remote/server.ts:254-271

**Issue:** broadcastEngineEvent iterates over the clients Map and sends to each WebSocket without checking if the connection is still open. Between the iteration start and send(), a client could disconnect, causing send() to throw.

**Impact:** Unhandled exceptions during engine event broadcast could crash the server or cause event loss to other connected clients when iteration is interrupted.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Check ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN before sending, or wrap send in try-catch and continue iteration on error. The send() method already has error handling but errors are silently swallowed which masks the issue.

title: [TUI/EventBridge]: Silent error swallowing in all dispatch wrappers destroys debugging
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/event-bridge.ts:264-310

**Issue:** All 8 dispatch wrapper functions (dispatchPhase, dispatchOutput, dispatchPipeline, dispatchHistory, dispatchTasks, dispatchUI, dispatchSubagent) silently swallow all errors with empty catch blocks. When a store dispatch fails, the error is completely lost, making it impossible to debug state corruption, store crashes, or data loss issues.

**Impact:** Critical failures in state management are hidden. When stores fail to update, the UI becomes inconsistent with actual state. Debugging requires adding manual instrumentation. Production issues are nearly impossible to diagnose.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add structured error logging with action context, store name, and error details. Consider using a central error handler that can report to telemetry while maintaining fault isolation.

title: [Coordinator]: Process leak in execGitIn and runCommand
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:2966-2992, 2994-3034

**Issue:** Child processes spawned via  are not explicitly killed or cleaned up on timeout/error. The  method has a timeout that kills the process, but  has no timeout mechanism and processes could hang indefinitely if git hangs. Neither method tracks or cleans up zombie processes.

**Impact:** Resource exhaustion from accumulated zombie/hung git processes, especially during long parallel runs with many git operations.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add timeout to execGitIn matching runCommand pattern, track spawned processes in a Set for cleanup during shutdown/dispose, ensure kill('SIGTERM') before SIGKILL.

title: [Remote]: Race condition between onerror and onclose handlers in client
description:
**Location:** src/remote/client.ts:288-314

**Issue:** The onerror handler rejects the connect promise and updates status, but onclose also fires and may schedule reconnection. This creates a race where both handlers execute, potentially causing double reconnection attempts or state inconsistencies.

**Impact:** Duplicate reconnection attempts, confusing event sequence (disconnected then reconnected events), or connection state machine corruption leading to 'ghost' connections that appear connected but are actually closed.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a single handler for both error and close events, or ensure proper synchronization with a guard flag (e.g., 'handled') to prevent double-processing.

title: [Plugins]: Race condition in BeadsTrackerPlugin getTasks refresh logic
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:450-453, 456-572

**Issue:** The refreshInFlight flag check at line 450-453 is not atomic. Between checking refreshInFlight and setting it at line 456, another async call can enter the method:
- Call A checks refreshInFlight (false)
- Call B checks refreshInFlight (false)
- Call A sets refreshInFlight = true
- Call B sets refreshInFlight = true
- Both execute getTasks simultaneously

**Impact:** Duplicate bd list commands, potential data inconsistency if beads are modified between calls.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a Promise-based lock or atomic compare-and-swap pattern.

title: [Remote]: Missing WebSocket state check in client send() method
description:
**Location:** src/remote/client.ts:333-337, 463-484

**Issue:** The send() and request() methods check _status === 'connected' but not the actual WebSocket readyState. The _status can become stale if the connection drops between the status check and the send() call.

**Impact:** Attempting to send on a closed WebSocket throws an error that propagates to callers unexpectedly, breaking the abstraction layer and potentially crashing calling code that doesn't expect throws from send().

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add explicit readyState check:  before any send operation.

title: [Plugins]: Unhandled promise rejection in BeadsTrackerPlugin sync() logging
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:762-811

**Issue:** The sync() method uses 'void' for async logging calls (lines 764, 782, 798), which suppresses unhandled promise rejection warnings but doesn't actually handle errors. If appendTrackerEvent throws, the error is silently swallowed.

**Impact:** Silent data loss of tracker events. If logging fails, there's no indication in the UI or logs.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Either await the logging calls (making sync() wait for them) or add proper .catch() handlers with console.error logging.

title: [TUI/Session]: Race condition between lock check and claim allows stale lock bypass
description:
**Location:** src/session/lock.ts:153-166, src/session/index.ts:280-291

**Issue:** checkLock() reads lock file and checks process status, but doesn't claim the lock atomically. Between checkLock() returning isStale=true and claimLockFile() executing, another process can claim the lock. This leads to both processes believing they acquired the lock.

**Impact:** Two Ralph instances can run concurrently in the same repo, causing state corruption, git conflicts, and data loss. The window is small but non-zero, especially under high system load or with slow filesystems.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Make lock acquisition atomic using file locking (flock) or rename-based compare-and-swap. The claim file mechanism attempts this but has a check-then-act race between readLockFile and rename operations.

title: [Remote]: Rapid reconnection loop on auth failure during reconnect
description:
**Location:** src/remote/client.ts:994-1001

**Issue:** When reconnection auth fails, the client clears the connection token and immediately schedules another reconnect without any delay. This can create a tight loop if the server consistently rejects auth (e.g., server restart with new token).

**Impact:** High CPU usage, network flooding, and potential server DoS from repeated connection attempts. The client appears to 'flicker' between connecting and disconnected states rapidly.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Respect the exponential backoff delay even on auth failure. Only clear connection token after exhausting retries, or use a longer initial delay for auth failures compared to network failures.

title: [Coordinator]: Stash leak on apply failure in main sync
[TUI/Session]: Silent JSON parse failure in session load causes data loss
description:
**Location:** src/session/persistence.ts:209-212, 254-259

**Issue:** readSessionMetadata and loadPersistedSession catch JSON parse errors and return null without any logging or error reporting. A corrupted session file (partial write, disk error, manual edit) is silently treated as no

title: [Remote]: Silent error swallowing masks connection and token issues
description:
**Location:** src/remote/server.ts:447-450, 1414-1419, token.ts:137-139

**Issue:** Multiple locations catch errors and silently ignore them with empty catch blocks or minimal logging. This makes debugging production issues extremely difficult and masks underlying problems.

**Impact:** Production issues require code modification to diagnose. Silent failures can accumulate (e.g., gradual connection leak) until catastrophic failure with no diagnostic trail.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add structured logging with severity levels: debug for expected cases (client disconnect), warn for unusual patterns (multiple consecutive failures), error for exceptions that shouldn't happen.

title: [Coordinator]: Sticky task affinity ignores blocked status
[Engine]: Silent error swallowing in emit() hides critical listener failures
description:
**Location:** src/engine/index.ts:385-393

**Issue:** The emit() method uses a bare try-catch that silently swallows ALL listener errors:


**Impact:** Critical bugs in event handlers are completely hidden. If a listener throws due to a bug, the engine continues as if nothing happened. This makes debugging impossible and can lead to silent data corruption or missed state updates.

**Pattern:** Bare catch block with no logging or error propagation.

**Fix Guidance:** At minimum, log errors to console.error with the event type and error message. Consider emitting an 'engine:error' event for listener failures to allow the TUI to display them.

title: [Coordinator]: Memory leak in active task tracking for blocked tasks
[Coordinator]: Premature taskFailureCounts deletion
[TUI/EventBridge]: Flush timer race condition allows event reordering and loss
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/event-bridge.ts:847-897

**Issue:** The flush() function clears flushTimer at the start, but scheduleFlush() only checks if flushTimer exists before creating a new one. If flush() is executing (cleared timer) and a new event arrives, scheduleFlush() creates a new timer. If flush() then takes longer than batchIntervalMs, the new timer fires while flush() is still processing, causing concurrent flushes. Events can be processed out of order or double-processed.

**Impact:** Engine events may be processed out of order causing UI state corruption. Output chunks may appear duplicated. Activity events may be lost if pendingEvents array is being iterated while new events are pushed.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a flush-in-progress flag or make flush() idempotent. Consider using a proper event queue with backpressure instead of timeout-based batching.

title: [Plugins]: Race condition in JsonTrackerPlugin template loading
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/json/index.ts:306-307, 332-357

**Issue:** Multiple concurrent calls to loadTemplateCache() can create multiple file read operations because the templateLoadPromise check and assignment are not atomic.

**Impact:** Unnecessary duplicate file reads during plugin initialization.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a single atomic check-and-set pattern or rely on the cached result.

title: [Remote]: Race condition in issueConnectionToken token cleanup
description:
**Location:** src/remote/token.ts:323-334

**Issue:** The issueConnectionToken function iterates over activeConnectionTokens to remove old tokens for the same client, then creates and adds a new token. This is not atomic - concurrent calls for the same client could result in multiple valid tokens or token loss.

**Impact:** Multiple valid tokens for same client increases attack surface. Or, old token is deleted after new one is added, causing the new token to be lost (client gets token that server doesn't recognize).

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a per-client mutex or redesign to store single token per client in a Map<clientId, token> structure instead of token-centric storage.

title: [TUI/OutputBuffer]: Unbounded Map growth for parallel outputs causes memory leak
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/output-buffer.ts:144-193

**Issue:** The parallelOutputs, parallelCliOutputs, and parallelSegments Maps grow unbounded as new task outputs are added. There's no eviction policy or cleanup mechanism. In long-running sessions with many tasks, this causes unbounded memory growth.

**Impact:** Memory leak in long-running sessions. With many parallel tasks or long iterations, the TUI process can consume excessive memory, potentially causing OOM crashes or system slowdowns.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add LRU eviction or max-size limits to parallel output Maps. Automatically clean up outputs when tasks complete or when the output is no longer being displayed. Consider adding a maxParallelOutputs configuration.

title: [Plugins]: Non-atomic file write in JsonTrackerPlugin.writePrd risks corruption
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/json/index.ts:517-531

**Issue:** The writePrd() method writes directly to the prd.json file. If the process crashes during write or if multiple instances write simultaneously, the file can be partially written or corrupted.

**Impact:** Data loss - prd.json can become corrupted, losing task completion status.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Write to a temporary file, then rename atomically. This ensures the file is never in a partially-written state.

title: [Remote]: Background migration errors only logged to console
description:
**Location:** src/remote/server.ts:1208-1225

**Issue:** The config push handler triggers auto-migration in background without awaiting and only logs errors to console. Migration failures are invisible to the client and audit log.

**Impact:** Silent migration failures leave remote in inconsistent config state. Client believes push succeeded but config may be incompatible. Console logs may be lost in production (daemon mode, log rotation).

**Pattern:**
[RemoteServer] Config migration failed...

**Fix Guidance:** Include migration status in push_config_response. Log migration failures to audit logger. Consider making migration synchronous (await) so client knows the final state.

title: [Plugins]: DroidAgentPlugin custom execute() bypasses base class execution tracking
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/droid/index.ts:139-296

**Issue:** DroidAgentPlugin completely overrides execute() instead of using the base class implementation. This means:
- It doesn't register executions in the executions Map
- It doesn't track currentExecutionId
- The interrupt(), getCurrentExecution(), interruptAll() methods from base class won't work properly
- Timeout handling and cleanup logic is duplicated and may drift from base class

**Impact:** Inconsistent behavior between agents. Droid executions can't be properly tracked or interrupted via base class methods.

**Fix Guidance:** Refactor to use base class execute() with appropriate overrides for stdin handling, or ensure all base class tracking mechanisms are properly maintained.

title: [TUI/SubagentStore]: Unbounded stats cache growth without eviction policy
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/subagent-store.ts:34-39, 140-162

**Issue:** The statsCache Map stores SubagentTraceStats keyed by iteration number. There's no limit on the number of entries and no eviction policy. In sessions with many iterations, this Map grows unbounded.

**Impact:** Memory leak in long-running sessions with many iterations. Each SubagentTraceStats contains tree structures and timing data that accumulates over time.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add LRU eviction with configurable max size (e.g., keep last 100 iterations). Or use a WeakMap if the iteration objects have appropriate lifecycle. Clear old stats when starting new runs.

title: [Coordinator]: Missing worker disposal cleanup on agent init failure
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:367-396

**Issue:** In , if  throws (line 390), the already-created worktrees and any successfully created workers are not cleaned up. The worktreeMap is created before the loop and worktrees are created even if later iterations fail.

**Impact:** Orphaned worktrees and worker resources on agent initialization failure.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Wrap in try/catch with cleanup of all created resources on failure, or use Promise.allSettled pattern to handle partial failures.

title: [Remote]: Directory creation errors silently ignored in push config
description:
**Location:** src/remote/server.ts:1179-1184

**Issue:** The mkdir call for config directory creation has an empty catch block that ignores all errors. If directory creation fails (permissions, disk full), the subsequent writeFile will likely fail with a confusing error message.

**Impact:** Users get 'Failed to write config' error when the real issue was directory permissions. Makes troubleshooting configuration push failures difficult.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Check error code - if EEXIST, continue silently; any other error should propagate or be logged with specific context about directory creation failure.

title: [Coordinator]: Validation batch timer may skip processing
[Plugins]: AgentRegistry.resetInstance() does not await disposal completion
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/registry.ts:82-96

**Issue:** The resetInstance() method calls instance.dispose() in a loop but only catches errors, never awaits them. The clear() operations may run before disposals complete, causing:
- Plugins still running after registry is 'reset'
- Resource leaks during test cleanup
- Potential race conditions in test suites

**Impact:** Test flakiness, resource leaks, potential hangs in test environments.

**Fix Guidance:** Make resetInstance() async and properly await all disposals before clearing maps.

title: [Coordinator]: Cooldown state not cleared when blocking task
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:1967-1971, 1134

**Issue:** `blockedTaskIds` is modified in both `handleMergeFailure` (add) and `markMergeSuccess` (delete). However, in `finalizeMergeSuccess`, when main sync fails, task is added to blockedTaskIds but `notReadyCooldowns` and `notReadyAttempts` are NOT cleaned up, potentially causing task to be picked up immediately in next iteration despite being blocked.

**Impact:** Race condition where blocked task could be picked up again before proper cooldown.

**Pattern:**
```typescript
this.pendingMainSyncTasks.set(entry.task.id, {...});
this.blockedTaskIds.add(entry.task.id); // Blocked
// notReadyCooldowns NOT cleared
```

**Fix Guidance:** Clear cooldown state when blocking task.

title: [Plugins]: TrackerRegistry.resetInstance() does not await disposal completion
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/registry.ts:82-96

**Issue:** Same issue as AgentRegistry - resetInstance() does not await disposal of instances before clearing maps.

**Impact:** Resource leaks, test flakiness.

**Fix Guidance:** Make method async and await disposals.

title: [TUI/Session]: Broad error swallowing in lock release may hide critical failures
description:
**Location:** src/session/lock.ts:270-289

**Issue:** deleteLockFile and deleteLockFileSync catch and silently ignore ALL errors, not just ENOENT. This hides permission errors, disk failures, or race conditions that could leave stale locks behind.

**Impact:** Stale locks may persist due to errors other than file

title: [TUI/UIStore]: Toast trimming race condition may lose notifications
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/ui-store.ts:571-582, 584-602

**Issue:** The push-toast and dismiss-toast reducers don't synchronize properly. If a push-toast and dismiss-toast action are dispatched concurrently, the dismiss may operate on stale state, causing the newly added toast to be incorrectly removed.

**Impact:** Users may miss important notifications (errors, completions) if they arrive during high UI activity. The toast queue state becomes inconsistent.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** The external store pattern should provide atomic updates. Consider adding a toast queue version or using functional updates. The issue is more likely in how components dispatch these actions concurrently.

title: [Plugins]: DroidAgentPlugin custom execute bypasses base class tracking
description:
**Bug:** DroidAgentPlugin custom execute bypasses base class tracking

**Location:** src/plugins/agents/droid.ts

**Issue:** DroidAgentPlugin implements custom execute that bypasses base class tracking mechanisms.

**Impact:** Execution tracking incomplete, metrics lost, inconsistent monitoring.

**Fix:** Ensure custom execute calls base class tracking methods, or align with base class pattern.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/plugins/agents/droid.ts | modify | Fix tracking bypass | Align with base class |

title: [Plugins]: BaseAgentPlugin executions Map can leak on completeExecution failure
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/base.ts:129-130, 481-531

**Issue:** In completeExecution(), if an error occurs after line 487 but before line 521, the execution is not removed from the executions Map. The error is only caught at line 488-493 with a debug log, but the Map entry remains.

**Impact:** Memory leak of execution objects, potential confusion if the same executionId is reused.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Wrap the complete execution logic in try-finally to ensure cleanup.

title: [TUI/PipelineStore]: Unbounded parallel timing Maps lack cleanup mechanism
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/pipeline-store.ts:56-67, 152-187

**Issue:** The parallelTimings and parallelIterations Maps store data for each parallel task but never clean up entries when tasks complete. In sessions with many parallel worktrees, these Maps grow unbounded.

**Impact:** Memory leak in parallel execution mode. With many worktrees and iterations, accumulated timing data consumes increasing memory.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add automatic cleanup when worktrees are pruned or tasks complete. Use LRU eviction or tie Map entries to worktree lifecycle. Consider clearing these Maps when the run completes.

title: [Engine]: Race condition in stop() allows concurrent execution
description:
**Bug:** Race condition in stop() allows concurrent execution

**Location:** src/engine/index.ts

**Issue:** Engine stop() doesn't properly synchronize, allowing concurrent execution during shutdown.

**Impact:** State corruption, partial execution, resource leaks during shutdown.

**Fix:** Add synchronization lock to stop() method, prevent new executions during shutdown.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Fix race condition in stop() | Add synchronization |

title: [Plugins]: BeadsTrackerPlugin claimTask race condition between lock and status update
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:735-756

**Issue:** In claimTask(), there's a race condition between creating the lock (line 736) and updating status to in_progress (line 749):
- Lock is created successfully
- Another process could claim the same task via bd CLI directly
- Status update succeeds anyway
- Both processes think they own the task

**Impact:** Double-processing of tasks, data corruption.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use atomic bd claim operation if available, or check lock is still owned before status update.

title: [Coordinator]: Partial conflict resolution leaves inconsistent state
[TUI/EventBridge]: Output capping performs expensive TextEncoder operations repeatedly
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/event-bridge.ts:796-811, output-buffer.ts:46-59

**Issue:** Both trimOutputToCap and trimToCap functions encode the entire output string to check byte length on every call. For large outputs near the 500KB cap, this performs O(n) work repeatedly as chunks are appended.

**Impact:** Performance degradation with large outputs. CPU usage spikes during high-output iterations, potentially causing UI frame drops or delayed responsiveness.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Track byte length incrementally as chunks are added, or use a more efficient data structure (like a ring buffer) that doesn't require re-encoding. Consider estimating UTF-8 byte length from string length for early-exit optimization.

title: [Coordinator]: Incomplete commit metadata on git show failure
[Coordinator]: Tasks not marked blocked on validation crash
[Coordinator]: Stale impact data in validation plan
description:
**Bug:** Stale impact data in validation plan

**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts

**Issue:** Impact data used for validation planning may be stale, causing incorrect validation decisions.

**Impact:** Incorrect validation, wrong task scheduling, out-of-date impact analysis.

**Fix:** Refresh impact data before validation, add freshness checks, use timestamps.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Fix stale impact data | Add freshness validation |

title: [Plugins]: O(n) getTask implementation in BaseTrackerPlugin is inefficient
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/base.ts:56-59

**Issue:** The default getTask() implementation fetches ALL tasks then filters by ID. For trackers with many tasks (1000+), this is inefficient.

**Impact:** Poor performance when looking up individual tasks, especially during dependency resolution loops.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Mark as abstract or require subclasses to override with efficient implementation. Current implementations (beads, json) do override but the base contract is inefficient.

title: [Plugins]: BeadsBvTrackerPlugin.bvAvailable flag not thread-safe
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads-bv/index.ts:325, 329-336, 415-421

**Issue:** The bvAvailable flag is checked at line 415 but could be modified by another call to initialize() while getNextTask() is running. There's no synchronization.

**Impact:** Race condition could cause fallback to beads behavior mid-operation or unexpected errors.

**Fix Guidance:** Use atomic check-and-use pattern or ensure flag is only set during initialization and never modified concurrently.

title: [TUI/Reconciliation]: Git process error handling is too broad, may miss failures
description:
**Location:** src/session/reconciliation.ts:49-79

**Issue:** execGit catches all errors in proc.on('error') but only appends to stderr. The exitCode is always 1 for errors, same as git command failure. There's no distinction between spawn failure (git not found) and git command failure.

**Impact:** If git is not installed or not in PATH, reconciliation silently fails with exitCode 1, same as if git returned an error. This makes troubleshooting environment issues difficult.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use different exit codes for spawn errors vs git command errors. Or include an error type field in the result. Log spawn errors distinctly from git stderr output.

title: [Plugins]: Unbounded message accumulation in Droid streaming parser
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/droid/outputParser.ts:693-701, 708-762

**Issue:** The MAX_STREAMING_MESSAGES limit is enforced by splicing the array, but this only happens after the limit is exceeded. During high-volume streaming, thousands of messages can accumulate before the splice occurs.

**Impact:** Memory spikes during heavy agent output, potential OOM in long-running operations.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a circular buffer or capped collection that doesn't allow temporary growth beyond limit.

title: [Coordinator]: Race condition in worker reservation and claim
description:
**Bug:** Race condition in worker reservation and claim

**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts

**Issue:** Worker reservation and claim are not atomic, allowing double-booking of workers.

**Impact:** Double-booked workers, task conflicts, inconsistent worker state.

**Fix:** Make reservation and claim atomic operation, add proper locking.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Fix worker reservation race | Atomic reservation/claim |

title: [TUI/PhaseStore]: Rate limit elapsed time calculation relies on system time
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/phase-store.ts:129-135, 137-152

**Issue:** The rateLimitElapsedSeconds calculation uses Date.now() directly without handling system time changes (NTP sync, user adjustment, daylight saving). If the system clock jumps backward, elapsed time becomes negative or incorrectly resets.

**Impact:** Rate limit UI shows incorrect elapsed time if system time changes. Users may see negative seconds or stuck counters. Not critical but confusing.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use a monotonic clock source (performance.now() or process.hrtime) for elapsed time calculations. Or handle negative results gracefully with a minimum of 0.

title: [Plugins]: BaseAgentPlugin.pendingInterrupt race condition
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/base.ts:300, 409-411

**Issue:** The pendingInterrupt flag is checked and set without synchronization. If interrupt() is called before startProcess() begins:
- pendingInterrupt is set to true
- Another thread could theoretically call interrupt() again
- The flag is checked after spawn but race window exists

**Impact:** Interrupt could be missed or double-processed, leading to zombie processes or failed interruption.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Use atomic operations or move interrupt handling into the process spawn to ensure it happens atomically.

title: [Plugins]: BeadsTrackerPlugin.getTask hydration error handling silently ignores failures
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:523-553

**Issue:** When hydrating dependencies (lines 524-537), if getTask() fails for a specific task, the error is silently caught and the hydrated task becomes undefined. The code at line 540-551 attempts to merge but doesn't validate the hydrated task exists.

**Impact:** Tasks may show as having no dependencies when they actually do, potentially allowing premature execution of blocked tasks.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Log errors during hydration and consider throwing if critical dependency info cannot be fetched.

title: [Plugins]: ClaudeAgentPlugin.parseClaudeOutputToEvents can hang on malformed JSON
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/builtin/claude.ts:418-425

**Issue:** The parseClaudeOutputToEvents() method splits data by newline and attempts to parse each line. If the input contains very long lines without newlines (malformed or attack input), this could cause memory issues.

**Impact:** Potential DoS via memory exhaustion if fed malicious output.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add line length limits or use streaming line parsing instead of splitting entire buffer.

title: [TUI/SubagentStore]: Unbounded stats cache growth without eviction policy
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/subagent-store.ts:34-39, 140-162

**Issue:** The statsCache Map stores SubagentTraceStats keyed by iteration number with no size limit or eviction policy. In sessions with many iterations, this Map grows unbounded, causing a memory leak.

**Impact:** Memory leak in long-running sessions with many iterations. Each SubagentTraceStats contains tree structures and timing data that accumulates over time.

**Pattern:** The set-stat reducer always adds entries but never removes them. Only remove-stat exists as manual cleanup.

**Fix Guidance:** Add LRU eviction with configurable max size (keep last 100 iterations). Or clear old stats when starting new runs.

title: [Plugins]: OpenCodeAgentPlugin JSON parsing has same DoS vulnerability
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/builtin/opencode.ts:95-102

**Issue:** Same pattern as Claude plugin - parseOpenCodeOutputToEvents splits entire data buffer by newline without length limits.

**Impact:** Potential memory exhaustion with malicious long-line input.

**Fix Guidance:** Add line length limits or use streaming parser.

title: [TUI/EventBridge]: Toast ID collision race condition under high-frequency events
description:
**Location:** src/tui/stores/event-bridge.ts:571-573 (via ui-store.ts reducer)

**Issue:** Toast IDs use Date.now() plus Math.random() which can collide when multiple toasts are created rapidly within the same millisecond. Colliding IDs cause incorrect deduplication or dismissal.

**Impact:** High-frequency events like rate limit warnings during parallel execution can cause toast notifications to be lost. Users miss critical warnings.

**Pattern:** Template literal with Date.now() and Math.random() is not collision-safe for high-frequency dispatch.

**Fix Guidance:** Use crypto.randomUUID() or a monotonic counter with timestamp prefix for guaranteed uniqueness. Maintain toast sequence number in store.

title: [Plugins]: Global templateCache in beads plugin has no invalidation
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:78

**Issue:** The templateCache is a global variable that persists across plugin instances. If the template.hbs file is modified, the cache never updates until process restart.

**Impact:** Template changes require full process restart to take effect.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Add cache invalidation mechanism or check file mtime.

title: [TUI/Session]: Incomplete error context in checkSession lock read failure
description:
**Location:** src/session/index.ts:218-235

**Issue:** When readLockFile throws an error other than ENOENT, checkSession logs a warning without the error code or type, making it impossible to distinguish permission errors from corruption or transient filesystem issues.

**Impact:** Troubleshooting lock issues is difficult. Cannot distinguish between permission problems, disk errors, or actual concurrent access scenarios.

**Pattern:** Only error.message is logged, missing error.code and error.stack.

**Fix Guidance:** Include error.code, error.name, and error.stack in log output. Classify errors: ENOENT=normal, EACCES/EPERM=permissions, other=corruption.

title: [Plugins]: execBd timeout handler can leave zombie processes
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:84-135

**Issue:** When a bd command times out, proc.kill('SIGTERM') is called but there's no wait for process exit. The process could become a zombie if it doesn't respond to SIGTERM.

**Impact:** Zombie process accumulation during long operations with timeouts.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Follow SIGTERM with a forced SIGKILL after grace period, similar to base agent implementation.

title: [Engine]: Stale currentExecution reference on error path
description:
**Bug:** Stale currentExecution reference on error path

**Location:** src/engine/index.ts

**Issue:** On error paths, currentExecution reference may become stale and reference completed/failed executions.

**Impact:** Stale data access, incorrect state reporting, potential crashes.

**Fix:** Clear currentExecution reference on error paths, add validation.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Fix stale currentExecution reference | Clear on error paths |

title: [Plugins]: BaseAgentPlugin completeExecution missing cleanup on error path
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/base.ts:481-531

**Issue:** If an exception occurs in completeExecution after retrieving the execution but before deleting from the Map (lines 499-521), the execution remains in the Map forever.

**Impact:** Memory leak of execution objects.

**Fix Guidance:** Wrap in try-finally to ensure Map cleanup always occurs.

title: [Plugins]: BeadsBvTrackerPlugin bv --robot-triage output parsing has no error handling
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads-bv/index.ts:443-451

**Issue:** JSON parsing of bv output (line 446) has a try-catch that falls back to base class, but if parsing fails halfway through a corrupted response, the triageOutput variable may be partially set.

**Impact:** Inconsistent state if fallback succeeds but uses stale/mixed data.

**Fix Guidance:** Clear triage state before attempting parse, or ensure atomic success/failure.

title: [Plugins]: Agent and Tracker registries allow plugin ID squatting
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/registry.ts:179-188, src/plugins/trackers/registry.ts:179-188

**Issue:** User plugins can override other user plugins, but built-in plugins are protected. However, if a user plugin is loaded first and then another user plugin with same ID is loaded, the second one silently overwrites the first.

**Impact:** User plugin conflicts resolved by last-wins, potentially confusing behavior.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Reject all duplicate IDs or warn when overwriting user plugins.

title: [Plugins]: JsonTrackerPlugin.getTasks swallows PrdJsonSchemaError details
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/json/index.ts:545-552

**Issue:** When a schema error occurs, logPrdSchemaError is called but the function returns empty array. The caller (engine) has no way to distinguish between 'no tasks' and 'schema error'.

**Impact:** Silent failures in task loading - TUI shows empty task list instead of error message.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Consider throwing a wrapped error or returning a result type that distinguishes error from empty.

title: [Plugins]: BeadsTrackerPlugin.removeTaskLock throws on unexpected errors
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:314-325

**Issue:** The removeTaskLock method only handles ENOENT (file not found) as a non-error case. Any other error (EPERM, EACCES, etc.) is re-thrown, potentially crashing the caller.

**Impact:** Unexpected crashes during task release operations.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Log and ignore all lock removal errors - the lock is advisory anyway.

title: [Plugins]: Droid outputParser isDroidJsonlMessage type guard insufficient
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/droid/outputParser.ts:765-767

**Issue:** The isDroidJsonlMessage type guard only checks for source === 'droid' but doesn't validate any other fields. A malformed object with just source can pass the check.

**Impact:** Type safety violations - code assuming DroidJsonlMessage structure may crash on malformed objects.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Validate at least one other required field (like type or raw).

title: [Plugins]: BeadsTrackerPlugin.knownTaskIds Set never cleared causes memory growth
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:258, 269-293

**Issue:** The knownTaskIds Set is initialized once and grows indefinitely as new tasks are discovered. Tasks that are completed or deleted are never removed.

**Impact:** Memory leak in long-running sessions with dynamic task creation.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Clear or reset the Set periodically, or use a bounded cache.

title: [Plugins]: BaseAgentPlugin interrupt() returns boolean without verifying process death
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/agents/base.ts:536-553

**Issue:** The interrupt() method returns true immediately after sending SIGTERM, without waiting for process termination. The caller has no way to know if the process actually died.

**Impact:** Race conditions where caller assumes process is dead but it's still running.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Return a Promise that resolves when process exits, or add isDead() check method.

title: [Plugins]: BeadsBvTrackerPlugin.refreshTriage has no error handling for execBv
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads-bv/index.ts:559-580

**Issue:** The refreshTriage() method calls execBv and parses output, but if the command fails (exitCode !== 0), the error is silently swallowed at line 570 and the triage state remains stale.

**Impact:** Stale triage data used for task recommendations, potentially leading to suboptimal task selection.

**Pattern:**


**Fix Guidance:** Log errors and consider clearing triage state on refresh failure.

title: [Plugins]: Memory leak in BeadsTrackerPlugin taskCache
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:259, 605

**Issue:** taskCache Map grows unbounded, never cleared.

**Fix:** Add LRU eviction or clear on sync.

title: [Coordinator]: Silent swallowing of listener errors
description:
**Bug:** Silent swallowing of listener errors

**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:115-124

**Issue:** Listener errors caught and silently ignored, hiding important failures.

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in parallel coordinator event listeners
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts:121-123

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Event listener errors are completely swallowed without logging. If a listener throws, the error is lost forever, making debugging impossible.

**Impact:** Silent failures in event handling can lead to missed state updates, lost task tracking, or incomplete cleanup operations without any indication of what went wrong.

**Fix:** At minimum log errors with context:

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in worktree git operations
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/worktree/git.ts:96-98

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Git operation failures are silently ignored. This is in the worktree creation/cleanup path which is critical for parallel execution isolation.

**Impact:** Failed git operations can leave worktrees in inconsistent states, causing subsequent operations to fail mysteriously or operate on wrong branches.

**Fix:** Log errors with context and potentially surface critical failures that could affect worktree integrity.

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent error swallowing in parallel engine event listeners
description:
**Location:** src/engine/parallel/engine.ts:101-102, 111-112

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Multiple locations in parallel engine swallow listener errors silently. These are the main entry points for task execution events.

**Impact:** Errors in task lifecycle event handling are lost, potentially hiding bugs in task scheduling, worker management, or state transitions.

**Fix:** Add error logging with event context to ensure listener failures are visible.

title: [Async]: Fire-and-forget void appendTrackerEvent calls lose errors
description:
**Location:** src/plugins/trackers/builtin/beads/index.ts:764,782,798, src/engine/index.ts:404,1019,1360, src/engine/parallel/engine.ts:329,369,449,470

**Pattern:** 


**Issue:** 12 instances of fire-and-forget void calls for critical event logging. Errors are silently lost. If tracker events fail to append, silent data loss occurs without any visibility.

**Impact:** Critical execution events (sync-start, sync-complete, iteration-failed) can be lost without detection. Makes debugging production issues impossible.

**Fix:** Add proper await with error handling, or at minimum add .catch() with error logging to console/error reporter.

title: [Error-Handling]: Silent swallowing of session persistence failures
description:
**Location:** src/session/persistence.ts:254-256, 291-293

**Pattern:**


**Issue:** Session persistence failures are logged at debug level only and silently return null/false. This affects session save/load operations.

**Impact:** Session data loss can occur without warning. Users may think sessions are persisted when they're not, leading to lost work and state inconsistencies.

**Fix:** Log persistence failures at error level with full context. Consider failing closed for critical session operations.

title: RC-002: Fix Active Task Lease Set Gap (.140)
description:
## Description
Active task lease set after claim creates a timing gap (bug .140). In the coordinator, the activeTasks set is updated after the task lease is claimed, creating a window where the task is leased but not tracked as active. This allows workers to claim tasks that appear available even though they are actively being processed, leading to potential duplicate execution or resource conflicts.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Ensure atomic update of lease + activeTasks set | Bundle lease claim and active tracking in single operation |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task lease claim and active task registration are atomic
- [ ] No gap exists between claiming lease and adding to activeTasks
- [ ] Concurrent workers cannot observe inconsistent task state
- [ ] Existing coordinator tests pass
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed active lease gap: Moved activeTaskLeases.set() into claimTask() under state lock. Now the tracker claim and lease recording are atomic with no window for race conditions. Build passes.

title: RC-011: Fix Plugin Registry Freshness Checks (.109)
description:
## Description
Plugin registry cached instances lack freshness checks (bug .109). The plugin registry caches plugin instances but does not verify the underlying plugin files have not changed. This can cause race conditions where stale plugin code is used after updates, leading to inconsistent behavior and potential security issues when plugins are hot-reloaded or modified.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/plugins/agents/registry.ts | modify | Add file hash/mtime validation | Check plugin freshness |
| src/plugins/agents/registry.ts | modify | Invalidate cache on file change | Auto-reload when plugin modified |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cache includes file modification time or hash
- [ ] Freshness check performed before returning cached instance
- [ ] Stale instances evicted and reloaded automatically
- [ ] No stale plugin code executed after file changes
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed plugin registry freshness: added validation in getInstance() to check cached instance plugin ID matches config.plugin, with automatic disposal and re-initialization of stale instances

title: RC-003: Fix Lock File Claim TOCTOU Race (.151)
description:
## Description
Lock file claim TOCTOU race condition (bug .151). When claiming tasks via lock files, the coordinator checks for file existence and then creates the lock file in separate operations. This creates a race window where two workers can simultaneously observe no lock file exists and both attempt to create one, resulting in both believing they successfully claimed the task.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Use atomic file operations for lock claiming | Bun's native atomic write or exclusive file open |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Lock file existence check and creation are atomic
- [ ] Exclusive file creation with O_EXCL or equivalent
- [ ] Concurrent lock attempts properly serialize with single winner
- [ ] Error handling for failed atomic operations
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed lock TOCTOU race - replaced check-then-write pattern with atomic tryWriteLockFileAtomic using O_CREAT | O_EXCL

title: RC-006: Fix Fire-and-Forget Void Calls (.1)
description:
## Description
Fire-and-forget void calls lose error context (bug .1). Several async void-returning function calls in the codebase are invoked without await or error handling, causing promise rejections to go unhandled. This loses critical error context and can cause race conditions when subsequent code assumes the operation completed successfully before it actually did.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Add await to all async void calls | Search for all void async invocations |
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Add await to all async void calls | Apply same pattern to engine |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All async void function calls are awaited
- [ ] Error handling added where rejections were silently ignored

description:
## Description
Stale currentExecution reference on error path (bug .8). When execution fails, the currentExecution reference may not be properly cleared or updated, leaving a stale reference that can be accessed by concurrent operations. This leads to race conditions where subsequent code operates on a failed or completed execution context, potentially accessing freed resources or corrupt state.

title: RC-012: Fix Template Cache Race Condition (.103)
description:
## Description
Template cache race condition in engine.ts (bug .103). The template compilation cache is accessed without proper synchronization, allowing concurrent compilation of the same template or read-during-write scenarios. This can cause template corruption, memory leaks from duplicate cached entries, or runtime errors from partially compiled templates.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Add synchronization for template cache | Mutex around get/compile operations |
| src/engine/index.ts | modify | Use compute-if-absent pattern | Atomic check-and-compile |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Template cache operations are thread-safe
- [ ] Single compilation per template under concurrent access
- [ ] No read-during-write on cached templates
- [ ] Cache lookup and compile are atomic

- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed template cache race: added compilationLocks Map for atomic check-and-set, double-checked locking pattern prevents duplicate compilation

title: RC-005: Fix Missing Await in drainPendingCoordinatorWork (.2)
description:
## Description
Missing await in drainPendingCoordinatorWork creates race (bug .2). The drainPendingCoordinatorWork function contains async operations that are not properly awaited, causing the function to return before work is complete. This allows subsequent operations to proceed while pending coordinator work is still in progress, leading to race conditions in task state and potential data loss or corruption.

### Impact Table

|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Add missing await keywords | Ensure all async operations are properly awaited |
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Verify return types are Promise<void> | Ensure async contract is explicit |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All async calls in drainPendingCoordinatorWork have await
- [ ] Function returns Promise that resolves only when work complete
- [ ] No fire-and-forget promises remain
- [ ] ESLint rule @typescript-eslint/no-floating-promises passes
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build

title: RC-001: Fix Task Lease Check-Then-Act Race (.110)
description:
## Description
Race condition in task lease check-then-act pattern in claimTask() at src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts. The bug .110 manifests as a Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability where the task lease status is checked, then acted upon in a non-atomic manner. Multiple workers can simultaneously pass the lease check before any single worker successfully claims the task, resulting in duplicate task execution or task state corruption.

### Impact Table
| Path | Change | Purpose | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| src/engine/parallel/coordinator.ts | modify | Add atomic compare-and-swap for task lease claims | Use atomic lock file operation or in-memory mutex |

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Task lease check and claim are performed atomically
- [ ] No TOCTOU window exists between lease check and claim
- [ ] Unit tests simulate concurrent claims and verify only one succeeds
- [ ] Existing coordinator tests pass
- [ ] bun run typecheck
- [ ] bun run lint
- [ ] bun run build
close_reason: Fixed task lease race: claimTask() now uses atomic check-and-set with acquireStateLock() - checking activeTaskLeases and setting the lease are now protected within the same locked section, preventing multiple workers from claiming the same task simultaneously.

title: CI-Parity Test Execution and Developer Workflow Hardening (20260208-125142)
description:
Real problem:
Running `bun test` monolithically in this repository can produce false negatives due to cross-suite `mock.module()` interference and mixed global state. CI already mitigates this by running test batches, but local developer flow does not have one canonical, documented command.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A single local command reproduces CI-style test batches and fails fast with a clear failing batch.
- [ ] CI and local execution share one source-of-truth batch runner to prevent drift.
- [ ] Contributor documentation clearly explains when to use batched tests vs targeted suites.
- [ ] Quality gates remain: typecheck, build, lint, and batched tests all pass for release-ready changes.
